title: RNA Isolation from Plant Tissue Protocol 11: Tri Reagent Method
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"14l6a","text":"Implemented by: Michael Melkonian and Barbara Surek","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"sqkd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f9ivt","text":"The protocol for RNA isolation using TRI Reagent (Molecular Research Center, Cincinnati OH) is analogous to the TRIzol LS Reagent protocol except that 1 ml of TRI Reagent is added to each 250 μl of homogenized material (= approx. 50–100 mg packed cell volume) (step 3, Protocol 10). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":37,"length":11},{"key":1,"offset":269,"length":11}],"data":[]},{"key":"6asks","text":"For background, TRI Reagent combines phenol and guanidine thiocyanate in a monophasic solution to inhibit RNase activity during RNA isolation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aqa7e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fhtr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6egk2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fl9m0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aro9r","text":"This protocol is part of a collection of eighteen protocols used to isolate total RNA from plant tissue. (RNA Isolation from Plant Tissue Collection: https://www.protocols.io/view/rna-isolation-from-plant-tissue-439gyr6) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":150,"length":69}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4BE9FD809AA811E9B188815C62D11156","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/protocol-11-tri-reagent-method-4ydgxs6/materials"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"C74805809AA811E9B188815C62D11156","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/protocol-10-trizol-ls-reagent-method-4rwgv7e"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"8DDCFE1913A9487181D79B5D31EFE4D0","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/rna-isolation-from-plant-tissue-439gyr6"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dobe9","text":"In cases of high RNA concentration (\u003e 1000 ng/μl), but low 260/230 ratio (less than 1), RNA can be extracted and precipitated again. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"78qu5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"39s4j","text":"For this, steps 8–13 of the TRIzol LS Reagent Method are repeated. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bqi9n","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"aidm1","text":"The RNA phase is mixed with   8 M LiCl by vortexing and incubating for   at  . Subsequently, steps 16–23 of the TRIzol LS Reagent protocol are performed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":28,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":71,"length":1},{"key":3,"offset":76,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"ebkmb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cvn4i","text":"Frequently, repetition of extraction decreases previous RNA concentrations up to tenfold, but increases the 260/230 ratio remarkably attaining values of 1.8 or more. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a282v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vsuk","text":"Neither using isopropanol for the second precipitation process nor replacing isopropanol by LiCl as a main precipitator is as efficient as the method described
